channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Air Zimbabwe Officially Launches Long-Awaited Direct Harare-London Route Today
item.description: Air Zimbabwe Relaunches Harare-London Route in Major International Comeback Zimbabwe’s national airline is taking a major step towards rebuilding its international network as it unveils its long-awaited London connection at Robert Gabriel Mugabe International Airport today. The ceremony comes ahead of the first Harare-London Gatwick flight, which is scheduled to take off on Wednesday, bringing [...] The post Air Zimbabwe Officially Launches Long-Awaited Direct Harare-London Route Today appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Bangladesh claim T20I consolation after Zimbabwe’s Test-ODI double @3menonaboat

item.title: Caledonia buoyed by improvement in access to higher-grade ore
item.description: Gold miner Caledonia Mining produced 17 360 oz of gold from its Blanket mine, in Zimbabwe, in the quarter ended June 30, an 18% increase from the 14 767 oz produced in the previous quarter, reflecting improving access to higher-grade mining areas. Grades delivered to the plant ha #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Australasia Global Mining News

item.title: Vaginal tightening herbs and guchu disaster: Men die during lula lula, Epworth woman dies while enlarging her genital ‘twins’
item.description: The Hidden Dangers: Unregulated Herbal Products and the Rising Toll on Health in Zimbabwe Zimbabwe is grappling with a silent health crisis as the unregulated market for traditional herbal products, particularly those marketed for intimate health and sexual enhancement, continues #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Churches rally behind CAA3 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Peter Matika CHURCHES have reaffirmed their commitment to promoting peace, national unity and constructive dialogue, saying Zimbabwe’s continued socio-economic transformation depends on citizens embracing harmony, tolerance and collective responsibility regardless of political or ideological differences. The call came after church leaders joined thousands of Zanu-PF supporters, party officials and residents at the Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3 (CAA3) Thank-You Rally held at Stanley Square in Makokoba on Sunday, where speakers repeatedly emphasised the importance of safeguarding the country’s long-standing culture of peace. Religious leaders who attended the gathering said the Church has a critical role to play in nurturing reconciliation, discouraging violence and fostering an environment in which citizens resolve differences through dialogue and mutual respect. Archbishop Dr Andby Makururu said the Church remains committed to preaching peace because stability is the bedrock upon which communities flourish and nations prosper. “The Church has always stood for peace because peace is a biblical principle and a national necessity. Whenever Zimbabweans live together in harmony, communities prosper, businesses grow and families thrive. We therefore encourage citizens to continue embracing dialogue, tolerance and mutual respect as the nation moves forward, he said. Archbishop Makururu said churches have a responsibility that extends beyond the pulpit by encouraging responsible citizenship, promoting unity and supporting initiatives that strengthen social cohesion. Our duty is not only to minister spiritually but also to help build peaceful communities. We urge Zimbabweans to reject violence, hate speech and actions that divide society. Instead, we must become ambassadors of reconciliation, love and nation-building because these values reflect both our Archbishop Makururu said constitutional processes should always be approached peacefully, allowing every citizen an opportunity to express their views without intimidation. “The participation of citizens in national processes demonstrates that people are willing to contribute positively to the country’s future. As churches, we encourage continued engagement through peaceful and lawful platforms because dialogue strengthens democracy while conflict weakens communities,” he said Archbishop Makururu also challenged young people to become champions of peace and development, saying they are central to Zimbabwe’s future. The future belongs to our young people. They should invest their energy in education, entrepreneurship, innovation and community development rather than allowing themselves to be manipulated into violence or destructive activities. Zimbabwe requires builders, not destroyers,” he said. Archbishop Makururu said churches across the country would continue partnering communities and national institutions in promoting moral values, peaceful coexistence and social responsibility. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: ‘Zimbabwe is not a banana republic’—Home Affairs minister warns plotters of planned July 31 protests over constitutional amendment
item.description: HOME Affairs and Cultural Heritage Minister Kazembe Kazembe has issued a stern warning to individuals planning demonstrations on July 31 against the recently enacted Constitutional Amendment Number 3 Act (CAA3), declaring that security agencies are prepared to thwart any attempts to disrupt public order. The planned protests have been spearheaded by opposition activists and constitutional [...] The post ‘Zimbabwe is not a banana republic’—Home Affairs minister warns plotters of planned July 31 protests over constitutional amendment appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Building Institutional Foundations for a Sustainable ZiG Single-Currency Regime: Beyond Currency Replacement Towards Financial System Transformation
item.description: Zimbabwe’s currency debate is often presented as a narrow argument about whether the country should continue using foreign currency or return fully to a domestic monetary system. However, the deeper issue is far more fundamental: it is about rebuilding the institutional foundations of a modern economy. By Brighton Musonza A successful transition to a mono-currency [...] The post Building Institutional Foundations for a Sustainable ZiG Single-Currency Regime: Beyond Currency Replacement Towards Financial System Transformation appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail
